title: acantholimon taschkurganicum var. taschkurganicum
intro: Acantholimon taschkurganicum var. taschkurganicum, also known as Taschkurgan's woolly thyme, is a flowering plant species native to Central Asia, belonging to the genus Acantholimon in the family Plumbaginaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to its high concentration of essential oils and phenolic compounds. The health benefits of Acantholimon taschkurganicum var. taschkurganicum include reducing inflammation and fighting off infections, as well as exhibiting antioxidant and antiseptic properties. In herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and its essential oil is used in aromatherapy and topical applications for skin and wound care.
